response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack and distributed systems experience with exposure to AI-agent infrastructure, but lacks explicit executive leadership in high-stakes fintech or regulated environments. His technical breadth and open-source contributions suggest potential, though the CTO role's scale and domain-specific demands require deeper validation.
strengths: Hands-on experience with event-driven architectures and agentic workflows (e.g., MCP at re:cinq, GISCollective platform).
Diverse technical background spanning backend, distributed systems, and real-time data (e.g., low-latency apps at Sociomantic, streaming at re:cinq).
Proven ability to build and maintain open-source tools (e.g., Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and contribute to developer tooling.
concerns: No explicit executive leadership experience (e.g., scaling teams from 10 to 30+, board-level communication).
Limited evidence of operating AI systems in regulated fintech contexts with financial consequences.
Agentic AI experience appears tangential (e.g., tooling contributions) rather than core architecture ownership.
